title: verbascum chaixii subsp. orientale
intro: Verbascum chaixii subsp. orientale, commonly known as the Turkish mullein, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mullein genus, Verbascum. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and sore throats. The health benefits of Verbascum chaixii subsp. orientale include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and act as a natural expectorant. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat a variety of ailments.
